channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Shiny Stories, Hidden Struggles: Investigating the Representation of Disability Through the Lens of LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2605.20191v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently attracted much attention for their ability to simulate human behavior and generate text that reflects personas and demographic groups. While these capabilities can open up a multitude of diverse applic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, arxiv, LLM, research, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Leveraging Large Language Models for Sentiment Analysis: Multi-Modal Analysis of Decentraland's MANA Token
item.description: arXiv:2605.20192v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentraland, a decentralized virtual reality platform operating within the expanding Metaverse ecosystem, utilizes its native MANA token to facilitate virtual asset transactions and governance. This study investigates the integration of Discord commu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, arxiv, research, analysis, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Improving Quantized Model Performance in Qualitative Analysis with Multi-Pass Prompt Verification
item.description: arXiv:2605.20193v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantized Large Language Models (LLMs) are used more often in qualitative analysis because they run fast and need fewer computing resources. This study examines how different lower bits quantization levels (8-bit, 4-bit, 3-bit, and 2-bit) and quantiza...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, arxiv, LLM, research, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Parallel LLM Reasoning for Bias-Resilient, Robust Conceptual Abstraction
item.description: arXiv:2605.20194v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have been increasingly used to analyze text. However, they are often plagued with contextual reasoning limitations when analyzing long documents. When long documents are processed sequentially, early or dominant concepts c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, experiment, LLM, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pseudo-Siamese Network for Planning in Target-Oriented Proactive Dialogues
item.description: arXiv:2605.20195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A target-oriented proactive dialogue system is designed to steer conversations toward predefined targets while actively providing suggestions. The core paradigm of such a system is to plan a reasonable dialogue path and subsequently guide language mod...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, transformer, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Under Pressure: Emotional Framing Induces Measurable Behavioral Shifts and Structured Internal Geometry in Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.20202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: I study whether emotionally framed evaluation follow-ups change both the behavior and the calm-relative internal representations of small, locally deployed language models. Our main benchmark uses Qwen 3.5 0.8B on four impossible-constraint coding tas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, arxiv, transformer, RAG, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Position: Let's Develop Data Probes to Fundamentally Understand How Data Affects LLM Performance
item.description: arXiv:2605.18801v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data is fundamental to large language models (LLMs). However, understanding of what makes certain data useful for different stages of an LLM workflow, including training, tuning, alignment, in-context learning, etc., and why, remains an open question....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, experiment, LLM, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Operationalizing Document AI: A Microservice Architecture for OCR and LLM Pipelines in Production
item.description: arXiv:2605.18818v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Academic research tends to focus on new models for document understanding creating a wide gap in the literature between model definition and running models at production scale. To close that gap, we present a microservice architecture that encapsulate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, LLM, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating the Utility of Personal Health Records in Personalized Health AI
item.description: arXiv:2605.18937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patient-managed Personal Health Records (PHRs) promises to empower patients to better understand their health; but information in the record is complex, potentially hindering insights. In this study, we assess the potential of large language models (L...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, template, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentNLQ: A General-Purpose Agent for Natural Language to SQL
item.description: arXiv:2605.19010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language to SQL (NL2SQL) conversion is an important problem for researchers and enterprises due to the ubiquitous importance of relational databases in broad-ranging practical problems. Despite the rapid advancements in the capabilities of LLM...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, research, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LEAP: A closed-loop framework for perovskite precursor additive discovery
item.description: arXiv:2605.20242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Efficient discovery of precursor additives is essential for improving the performance of perovskite solar cells, yet the large chemical space makes conventional trial-and-error screening inefficient. We develop LEAP(LLM-driven Exploration via Active L...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, LLM, framework, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GROW: Aligning GRPO with State-Action Modeling for Open-World VLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.20246v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, vision-language model (VLM) agents have shown promising progress in open-world tasks, where successful task completion often requires multiple turns of visual perception and action execution. However, existing methods still rely primarily on...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, demonstration, prompt, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph Transductive Sharpening: Leveraging Unlabeled Predictions in Node Classification
item.description: arXiv:2605.20248v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the transductive setting, where the full graph is observed but node labels are only partially available, progress in semi-supervised node classification has largely focused on architectural innovation. In this paper, we revisit an orthogonal axis: ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, alignment, RAG, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learn-by-Wire Training Control Governance: Bounded Autonomous Training Under Stress for Stability and Efficiency
item.description: arXiv:2605.19008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language-model training is increasingly exposed to instability, degraded runs, and wasted compute, especially under aggressive learning-rate, scale, and runtime-stress conditions. This paper introduces Learn-by-Wire Guard (LBW-Guard), a bounded...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, LLM, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GraphDiffMed: Knowledge-Constrained Differential Attention with Pharmacological Graph Priors for Medication Recommendation
item.description: arXiv:2605.20188v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recommending safe and effective medication combinations from electronic health records (EHRs) is a core clinical AI problem, yet it remains difficult because patient trajectories are long, noisy, and clinically heterogeneous. Existing methods typicall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, arxiv, experiment, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FlowLM: Few-Step Language Modeling via Diffusion-to-Flow Adaptation
item.description: arXiv:2605.20199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present FlowLM, a flow matching language model transformed from pre-trained diffusion language models via efficient fine-tuning. By re-aligning the curved sampling trajectories of diffusion models into straight-line flows, FlowLM enables high quali...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Interference-Aware Multi-Task Unlearning
item.description: arXiv:2605.19042v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine unlearning aims to remove the contribution of designated training data from a trained model while preserving performance on the remaining data. Existing work mainly focuses on single-task settings, whereas modern models often operate in multi-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, framework, vision, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DecisionBench: A Benchmark for Emergent Delegation in Long-Horizon Agentic Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2605.19099v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce DecisionBench, a benchmark substrate for emergent delegation in long-horizon agentic workflows. The substrate fixes a task suite (GAIA, tau-bench, BFCL multi-turn), a peer-model pool (11 models, 7 vendor families), a delegation interface ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, arxiv, release, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
